data.title: Virgin Galactic rises after six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
Virgin Galactic (NYSE: SPCE) traded higher on Thursday afternoon, rising 2.36% to $2.39 and snapping a six-session losing streak.

The stock has dropped more than 20.21% over the past six trading days, underperforming the S&P 500 Index, which gained 0.38% over the same period. Year to date in 2026, shares are down over 25.70%, compared with a 4.25% advance in the broader index.

In the prior month, the company priced spaceflight tickets at $750,000, about $100,000 higher than earlier offerings, as it focuses on developing a more reusable spacecraft after halting commercial flights in June 2024. CEO Michael Colglazier said the first SpaceShip is nearing completion, with ground testing set to begin in April, while a second is expected to enter service in late 2026 or early 2027.

The update follows quarterly results that missed expectations, with the company reporting a loss of $0.98 per share as revenue fell 27.9% year over year to about $312,000.

According to Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings, SPCE carries a “Hold” rating with a score of 2.73 out of 5, reflecting a D- grade for profitability, alongside an A- for growth and an A for valuation.

Wall Street analysts also remain cautious on the stock, assigning it a “Hold” rating.

 However, analyst Bernard Zambonin maintains a more constructive view, calling SPCE a high-risk, high-reward play with potential upside if key execution milestones, including a planned commercial flight in Q4 2026, are achieved.

Similarly, Seeking Alpha analysts appeared rather bullish about the stock, issuing a Buy call for SPCE.

data.title: Bleeding disorder firm Hemab upsizes proposed IPO to 15M shares
data.description:
• Hemab Therapeutics Holdings (COAG), a drug developer targeting blood coagulation disorders, upsized its initial public offering, planning to issue 15M shares of its common stock at $16–$18 per share under the ticker symbol “COAG.” On Nasdaq.
  • The clinical-stage biotech whose backers include the Novo Nordisk Foundation, the controlling shareholder of weight loss drug developer Novo Nordisk (NVO), previously sought to sell approximately 11.8M shares.
  • According to a regulatory filing on Thursday, the Cambridge, Massachusetts-based company has expanded the offering to 15M shares, planning to raise $270M at a valuation of roughly $764M at the top of the price range.
  • Hemab (COAG) intends to use the IPO proceeds primarily to advance its lead candidate, sutacimig, which is currently undergoing early/mid-stage development for two genetically driven bleeding disorders called Glanzmann thrombasthenia and Factor VII deficiency.

data.title: Adamas Trust downgraded at B. Riley Securities despite blowout earnings
data.description:
Adamas Trust (ADAM) was downgraded at B. Riley Securities even as the mortgage REIT's Q1 earnings beat the consensus by a wide margin.

"Investment activity continues to be strong at ADAM, with single-family credit growing 5.6% Q/Q while agency grew 1.4%, which is a divergence from 2025 investing, which was predominantly through the agency channel," said analyst Timothy D'Agostino.

"Given the overall growth in 2025 and 1Q26, including a new investment focus, the closing of Constructive, and an increase to the quarterly dividend in 3Q25, the stock is up 57.5% over the TTM (RUT 39.5%)," said the analyst.

"Given the price action, we move to the sideline and await a better entry point. We tick up our PT (price target) to $9.50 to reflect the increase in BV (book value)," said the research note.

"We are long-term supporters of the platform and believe management has done an excellent job transitioning the investment portfolio, which has led to EAD expansion and a quarterly dividend increase in 3Q25," D'Agostino noted.

B. Riley cut its recommendation on ADAM to Neutral from Buy, but raised the price target to $9.50 from $9.00.

The rating aligns with the average Seeking Alpha authors rating of Hold. Meanwhile, the sell-side analysts grade the stock as Buy.

data.title: Activist Jana renews push for Markel to divest ventures, buy back shares - report
data.description:
Activist Jana Partners is renewing a push for Markel Group (MKL) to divest its venture arm, and it also wants the insurer to repurchase $2 billion of its stock. 

“The current structure produces sub-peer shareholder returns, creates no unique value and warrants a discounted multiple,” Jana Managing Partner Scott Ostfeld and Managing Director Jimmy Ganas said in a letter to Markel’s board Thursday reviewed by Bloomberg News. “The need for change is clear.” 

The renewed push comes after Jana said in December 2024 that Markel (MKL) should explore a separation or sale of its private investments business., Markel Ventures. 

Jana and Markel didn't immediately respond to Bloomberg's request for comment.

Markel (MKL) reported Q1 results on Tuesday.

Shares of Markel rose 1.1% and have dropped 18% this year.

data.title: TGS ASA reports Q1 results
data.description:
• TGS ASA press release (TGSNF): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.05.
  • Revenue of $320.5M (-28.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Labcorp forecasts 2026 adjusted EPS of $17.70 to $18.35 while raising revenue growth outlook to 5% to 6.1%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Labcorp (LH) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "We are off to a strong start in 2026 with continued momentum in both our Diagnostics and Central Laboratory businesses" (President, CEO & Chairman Adam Schechter), adding that "revenue reached $3.5 billion, increasing 6%" and that "adjusted earnings per share grew 11%."
  • "We recently announced a nationwide strategic collaboration with Children's Hospital of Philadelphia to expand access to cutting-edge diagnostics for pediatric patients" (President, CEO & Chairman Schechter), and said Labcorp "completed our acquisition of select assets of Crouse Health's Laboratory Alliance of Central New York" and "remain on track to close our acquisition of select outreach laboratory services across Indiana and Northwest Ohio from Parkview Health in the very near future."
  • "We continue to deliver double-digit growth" in Consumer Health (President, CEO & Chairman Schechter), highlighting that "MyLabcorp, our secure mobile app" is "launching in May" and that "MyLabcorp's AI assistant will also help simplify appointment scheduling and payments."
  • "In the first quarter, enterprise revenue grew 5.8% and enterprise adjusted operating margin expanded more than 30 basis points to 14.4%" (CFO & Executive VP Julia Wang), adding "Adjusted EPS was $4.25" and "we generated $71 million in free cash flow."

Outlook

  • "We are raising the midpoint of the enterprise revenue range by approximately $30 million and the midpoint of the EPS range by $0.13" (CFO & Executive VP Wang).
  • "We expect enterprise revenue to grow 5% to 6.1%" (CFO & Executive VP Wang), while "we expect Diagnostics segment revenue to grow 5.1% to 5.9%" and "we expect BLS segment revenue to grow 3.8% to 5.4%."
  • "Our adjusted EPS guidance range is $17.70 to $18.35" (CFO & Executive VP Wang), and "our free cash flow guidance range remains $1.24 billion to $1.36 billion, weighted towards the second half of the year."

Financial Results

  • "Enterprise revenue was $3.5 billion" with "3.1% organic growth" and "adjusted operating income was $508 million" (CFO & Executive VP Wang).
  • "Diagnostic Laboratories delivered another strong quarter with 5% revenue growth to $2.8 billion" (CFO & Executive VP Wang), stating "Total volume growth was 2.5%" and "Price/mix increased 2.6%."
  • "Biopharma Laboratory Services revenue grew to $781 million, up 8.2%" and "our BLS segment ended the quarter with a backlog of $8.6 billion" (CFO & Executive VP Wang), adding "we expect approximately $2.7 billion to convert into revenue over the next 12 months."

Q&A

  • Lisa Gill, JPMorgan Chase & Co.: Asked about weather impacts and ACA exchange changes; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter said, "we estimate it was about a $15 million impact," while CFO & Executive VP Wang said the ACA effect in Q1 was "really immaterial" but added, "we continue to believe that the 30 basis point volume impact is a good estimate to work with."
  • Jack Meehan, Nephron Research: Asked about CMS "CRUSH" exposure; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter said, "we're supportive of any initiative that can create a level playing field" and added ACLA urged CMS "to avoid unintended consequences" including "impeding Medicare patient access."
  • Michael Cherny, Leerink Partners: Asked about Diagnostics volume vs. mix and share; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter tied mix to specialty growth and said, "I feel confident in the guidance that we've provided," while CFO & Executive VP Wang said, "we continue to expect the majority of that revenue growth to be coming from organically."
  • Patrick Donnelly, Citi: Asked about BLS bookings and confidence in sequential book-to-bill improvement; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter said the Q1 "0.94" book-to-bill was "mostly driven by timing" and added, "I expect to have a book-to-bill that will remain above 1" for the year.
  • Tycho Peterson, Jefferies: Asked about esoteric testing reimbursement and Alzheimer’s testing channel strategy; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter said oncology reimbursement "aren't necessarily quite where we'd like them to be" and added on Alzheimer’s, "we will break it out because it is growing so quickly" (while noting, "We don't yet break out the individual segments").
  • Elizabeth Anderson, Evercore ISI: Asked about PAMA survey participation and RESULTS Act; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter said, "we're really waiting for a CBO score" and emphasized PAMA impact depends on reporting: "The more that report, the lower the impact will be for Labcorp."
  • David Westenberg, Piper Sandler: Asked about consumer investments and ROI; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter said, "we already do some advertising" and added, "there are some parts of it... we're not necessarily going to compete at this time" due to pricing uncertainty.
  • Michael Ryskin, BofA Securities: Asked about LaunchPad and margin pacing; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter said, "we're on track," and CFO & Executive VP Wang said, "we continue to expect another year of margin improvement in Diagnostics" and that "BLS margin" is expected "to improve more than that for Diagnostics."
  • Anna Kruszenski, Barclays: Asked about fuel inflation and further weather assumptions; CFO & Executive VP Wang said the fuel AOI impact estimate is "approximately $5 million to $10 million this year" and added, "we generally do not bake in explicit assumption for weather."
  • Erin Wright, Morgan Stanley: Asked about the deal pipeline and comparisons to last year; President, CEO & Chairman Schechter said, "Our pipeline remains very strong" and "I wouldn't say it's accelerated versus this time last year."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were slightly negative to neutral, emphasizing policy and utilization uncertainty ("potential changes" to ACA exchanges; "CRUSH" exposure; PAMA participation) and near-term levers (bookings, margins, fuel, weather).
  • Management tone was slightly positive and confident in execution and guidance, with repeated language such as "I feel good about" and "I feel confident" (President, CEO & Chairman Schechter) and "we are really pleased" and "we continue to expect" (CFO & Executive VP Wang).
  • Compared with the prior quarter, management’s confidence language was more explicitly tied to raised guidance ("raising the midpoint") versus Q4’s framing of an outlook that "reflects the delay of PAMA through December 31, 2026" (President, CEO & Chairman Schechter in Q4 2025).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted upward: Q1 raised 2026 midpoints ("approximately $30 million" for revenue and "$0.13" for EPS), while Q4 initially guided to "revenue to grow 4.7% to 6%" and adjusted EPS "$17.55 to $18.25" (CFO & Executive VP Wang in Q4 2025).
  • Analysts’ policy focus evolved from PAMA delay mechanics in Q4 to practicalities of upcoming reporting and related initiatives in Q1 (PAMA survey timing; "CRUSH" fraud-and-abuse initiative).
  • Strategic emphasis stayed consistent (hospital partnerships, specialty diagnostics, AI/automation), with Q1 adding more explicit commercialization and consumer-interface milestones, including "MyLabcorp" launching "in May" (President, CEO & Chairman Schechter).

Risks and Concerns

  • "We estimate [weather] was about a $15 million impact" (President, CEO & Chairman Schechter), with recapture limits acknowledged for physician-office testing: "There, we don't necessarily know who has appointments" (President, CEO & Chairman Schechter).
  • "We continue to believe that the 30 basis point volume impact is a good estimate" from ACA dynamics (CFO & Executive VP Wang), while noting Q1 impact was "immaterial" and dependent on premiums paid and utilization.
  • On reimbursement and policy, management flagged uncertainty around PAMA outcomes and participation: "The impact to Labcorp will be highly dependent upon the number of other laboratories, including hospital laboratories that report their data" (President, CEO & Chairman Schechter).

Final Takeaway

Management described Q1 as a "strong start" supported by Diagnostics and Central Labs momentum, margin expansion, and continued capital deployment, and it raised 2026 guidance midpoints while reiterating focus on hospital partnerships, specialty testing growth, and technology-driven efficiencies. In Q&A, analysts concentrated on policy-driven volume and pricing variables (ACA exchanges, CRUSH, PAMA/RESULTS Act) and near-term operational sensitivities (weather, fuel, bookings), while management repeatedly characterized guidance and execution as on track and monitored risks as "manageable" within the updated 2026 ranges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: CNX outlines fuller Utica readout by late 2026/early 2027 while convertible converts into ~12 million shares this week
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CNX Resources (CNX) Q1 2026

Management view

  • On the Utica program, CEO Alan Shepard said the latest pad was “a recent TIL towards the last part of the quarter” and that CNX is “a little ways off from providing any sort of production results from that,” adding that performance so far is “very consistent with what our expectation of the reservoir is” and cost work is continuing, but “nothing new to update at this time.” (President, CEO & Director Alan Shepard)
  • Shepard framed timing for a broader update, saying, “probably towards the end of this year, we’ll be in a position to provide more fulsome and we’ll have a nice data set to provide to the market towards the end of ’26, early ’27 once these wells have had enough duration on.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • On capital allocation between plays, Shepard said, “the Marcellus has the advantage of having the infrastructure already there,” and CNX will “optimize for kind of the best economics per well,” adding, “you will see us kind of blend in more Utica over time,” while “the Swift of Marcellus, we’re in harvest mode there, and you’re going to continue to see those for the next few years.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • On the “new tech business,” Shepard said, “everything is consistent with where we thought it’d be at this point in ’26,” and added, “we’re still waiting for, sort of, the final guidance on 45Z, but we don’t think that’s going to impact any of the projections we’ve made so far.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • CFO Everett Good highlighted balance-sheet actions, saying, “we did a very positive refinancing of our 2029 notes at new 8-year notes at 5 and 7/8s in the quarter,” and reiterated CNX’s approach: “we try to push out the maturities to make sure that we’re at least 2, 3 years out before our next maturity.” (Chief Financial Officer Everett Good)

Outlook

  • The call did not include explicit quarterly or full-year EPS, revenue, or CapEx guidance figures; management directed listeners to written prepared remarks posted online, and the live event was “used exclusively for Q&A.” (VP of Investor Relations Tyler Lewis)
  • On long-dated hedging, Good said CNX is “in a position to be more opportunistic maybe than we have in the past and patient,” adding, “we’ve seen basis differentials tighten as well, and that’s really helped us get to a better all-in realized price in kind of the Cal 28 market,” while “we’re targeting to bring that up over time as we approach that year.” (Chief Financial Officer Good)
  • On in-basin demand, Shepard said CNX shares “the same sort of long-term optimism on the demand side,” but flagged uncertainty on schedule: “the only question in my mind is timing, right? Is it 3 years? Is it 5 years? Is it 7 years?” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)

Financial results

  • No income statement, cash flow, EPS, or revenue figures were discussed on the live Q&A call; management referenced that “detailed first quarter earnings release data such as quarterly E&P data, financial statements and non-GAAP reconciliations” were posted to the investor relations site. (VP of Investor Relations Lewis)
  • On capital structure items affecting share count, Good said the remaining convertible maturity “is on May 1 of this week,” and “the shares will be issued about approximately 12 million shares net issuance later this week,” while Shepard added, “when we say net, that’s included the effect of the capped call.” (Chief Financial Officer Good; President, CEO & Director Shepard)

Q&A

  • Leo Mariani, ROTH: asked for an Utica update on “well performance or costs”; President, CEO & Director Shepard answered that it is “a little ways off” for production results, that what CNX has seen is “very consistent,” and that a “nice data set” is expected “towards the end of ’26, early ’27.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • Mariani, ROTH: asked whether Utica could get more allocation than Marcellus; Shepard responded that Marcellus benefits from legacy infrastructure, CNX will “blend in more Utica over time,” but Marcellus remains in “harvest mode.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • Mariani, ROTH: asked for updates on tech initiatives including “AutoSep” and “CNG or LNG”; Shepard replied, “nothing new to update,” while noting 45Z guidance is pending but “we don’t think that’s going to impact any of the projections.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • Jacob Roberts, TPH: asked what CNX is seeing in the “2028 market” and referenced CNX adding “another 13 Bcf” to hedges; CFO Good said CNX is being “opportunistic” and “patient,” with tightened basis helping “a better all-in realized price” in “Cal 28.” (Chief Financial Officer Good)
  • Roberts, TPH: asked about next steps on the balance sheet; Good cited refinancing “2029 notes” into “new 8-year notes at 5 and 7/8s,” and said the next maturity is “a 2030 maturity that we’ll handle well ahead of time.” (Chief Financial Officer Good)
  • Michael Scialla, Stephens: asked about in-basin demand growth and how CNX can capture it; Shepard said announcements are “mind-boggling,” CNX is “participating” in RFPs, and the key uncertainty is “timing.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • Scialla, Stephens: asked whether demand develops more in Ohio vs. Pennsylvania; Shepard said CNX is “pretty agnostic,” added Ohio is “a little easier to do business with in terms of speed,” and pointed to Pennsylvania projects “in our footprint,” while also noting hoped-for continued tightening differentials “in the out years.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • Scialla, Stephens: asked timing for the remaining convertible conversion and Q2 diluted shares; Good said maturity is “on May 1 of this week” with “approximately 12 million shares net issuance later this week,” and Shepard linked “net” to the “capped call.” (Chief Financial Officer Good; President, CEO & Director Shepard)

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral to slightly positive, centered on longer-dated hedging, debt steps, and demand visibility, with explicit uncertainty embedded in questions such as “can you give some context on what you’re seeing in that 2028 market?” and “can you say when during the quarter you expect that remaining…to convert?” (Jacob Roberts, TPH; Michael Scialla, Stephens)
  • Management’s tone was slightly positive but cautious on timing, with Shepard emphasizing long-term demand optimism but repeatedly returning to schedule uncertainty: “the only question in my mind is timing,” while Good stressed patience in hedging: “more opportunistic… and patient.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard; Chief Financial Officer Good)
  • Versus Q4 2025, the Q1 2026 live call was more narrowly transactional and timing-focused (Utica readout timing, Cal 28 hedging, refinancing, convert issuance), while the prior quarter included broader operational and market framing such as CapEx/production cadence and regulatory/infrastructure constraints. (President, CEO & Director Shepard; Chief Financial Officer Good)

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • The Q1 2026 call format remained Q&A-only with prepared remarks posted separately, but unlike Q4 2025, there were no opening comments from Shepard; Q4 began with Shepard stating, “We normally don’t provide opening remarks on these calls,” before addressing the “extraordinary cold weather event.” (President, CEO & Director Alan Shepard)
  • Utica messaging shifted from operational detail in Q4 to timing and data-set readiness in Q1: in Q4, COO Navneet Behl said, “our average Utica cost is about $1,700 per foot” and described “spacing evaluation” with “1,300-foot spacing” and “1,500-foot spacing,” while in Q1 Shepard emphasized being “a little ways off” from production results and pointed to “end of ’26, early ’27” for a fuller market update. (Chief Operating Officer Navneet Behl; President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • In-basin demand discussion intensified in Q1 2026 with Shepard citing “multiple” proposed “9-gigawatt” plants and active participation in RFPs; Q4 focused more on not “chasing” spot pricing and waiting for durable “’27, ’28, ’29 strip” signals tied to “new infrastructure, new power plants.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • Balance-sheet updates were more explicit in Q1 2026, with Good describing refinancing terms and near-term share issuance mechanics tied to the convert maturity; Q4’s live discussion did not include an equivalent specific convert timing statement. (Chief Financial Officer Good)

Risks and concerns

  • Shepard flagged uncertainty around in-basin demand timelines: “The only question in my mind is timing, right? Is it 3 years? Is it 5 years? Is it 7 years?” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • On policy-related uncertainty, Shepard said CNX is “still waiting for, sort of, the final guidance on 45Z,” while stating it is not expected to change projections. (President, CEO & Director Shepard)
  • On Utica transparency near term, Shepard said CNX is “a little ways off from providing any sort of production results,” delaying near-term performance validation until wells have “enough duration on.” (President, CEO & Director Shepard)

Final takeaway

Management emphasized long-term demand optimism in Appalachia and tighter out-year differentials, while repeatedly underscoring timing uncertainty for both in-basin power/data-center buildouts and Utica well-readouts. In parallel, the company highlighted balance-sheet execution via a refinancing of 2029 notes into new 8-year notes at 5 and 7/8s and guided to a near-term increase in share count from a May 1 convertible maturity, with approximately 12 million net shares expected to be issued later in the week, inclusive of the capped-call structure.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Rolls-Royce surges after maintaining profit guidance following strong YTD
data.description:
Rolls-Royce (RYCEY) (RYCEF) up 8.4% in Thursday's trading after reiterating its full-year guidance for profit to ‌rise at least 16% following a strong start to the year and saying it expects to fully mitigate the current financial impact of the disruption from the Middle East war.

"We have had a strong start to the year driven by our transformation and self-help, as we continue to further expand the earnings, cash, and growth potential of the business," CEO Tufan Erginbilgic said, adding it is "proactively mitigating the impact associated with the conflict in the Middle East."

The company said it has greater confidence in its guidance as a result, and continues to expect full-year underlying operating profit of £4B-£4.2B ($5.39B-$5.66B) with free cash flow of £3.6B-£3.8B, while it remains well positioned to deliver its midterm targets with substantial growth beyond the midterm from both its existing and new businesses.

Rolls-Royce (RYCEY) (RYCEF) also said usage of its Trent XWB engines has returned to pre-war levels in the region, and that capacity cuts have predominantly hit the narrowbody market.

data.title: eBay's Q2 GMV deceleration unnerves investors, but sell-side sees a buying opportunity
data.description:
EBay’s (EBAY) first-quarter results delivered on revenue and profit growth as well as gross merchandise value ((GMV)) and surprised to the upside versus Wall Street’s expectations.

But shares are moving lower, putting the stock at risk of ending the week in the red, as the deceleration in GMV for the second quarter spooked investors as one-time benefits fade and comparisons become more challenging.

While this possible outcome has unnerved investors, sell-side analysts remain confident in eBay’s (EBAY) ability to outperform, creating an attractive buying opportunity at the current share price.

“We believe second half GMV will outperform with momentum across segments, new growth vectors, historical conservatism, and underappreciated AI upside,” says Morgan Stanley’s Nathan Feather.

“It’s clear to us from the results from eBay (EBAY) and Etsy (ETSY) that e-commerce marketplaces are showing the ability to leverage AI to help buyers more easily find the right goods across their vast listings,” adds Needham’s Bernie McTernan.

In addition to GMV deceleration, shares are also weighed down Thursday by unadjusted operating margin compression of 380 basis points due to higher costs and transaction losses.

However, Needham’s bull case for eBay (EBAY) assumes more efficient customer acquisition that will lead to greater operating leverage and margin expansion. 

Morgan Stanley is also confident in the company’s ability to deliver on margin expansion through GMV growth and take rate expansion.

“We believe eBay has returned to durable GMV growth through an improved buyer experience, stacking many small initiatives along with an easing competitive environment,” Feather says, reiterating an Overweight rating on the stock with a $121 price target, representing 16% upside to Wednesday’s closing price.

While the stock is down 2% on Thursday, eBay (EBAY) shares are up 45% year-over-year, outperforming the S&P 500 by 13%.

data.title: Bandwidth hits 52-week high after Q1 revenue beat: Full outlook
data.description:
Bandwidth (BAND) is turning into one of the market's hottest AI-driven telecom winners after a post-earnings breakout. The stock surged nearly ~37% in a single session to around ~$33.21 after recording stronger-than-expected Q1 FY26 revenue.

The rally also pushed the stock to a fresh 52-week high while raising its full-year outlook. The shares are now up nearly ~85% in one month and ~118.64% YTD.

This momentum outperformed the S&P 500 (SP500) index’s 4.25% YTD gain. Even on a one-day chart, the company gained 0.23% vs. the S&P 500’s 0.01%.

According to the official release, Q1 revenue came in at $209M, up 20.1% Y/Y while beating estimates by $7.43M. Adjusted EBITDA rose 17% to $26M, although non-GAAP EPS of $0.13 missed expectations by $0.17.

The company also lifted FY26 revenue guidance to $880M-$900M, above consensus estimates. Adding to this scenario, CEO David Morken said AI voice adoption is accelerating, highlighting Bandwidth’s partnership with Salesforce Agentforce and growing million-dollar enterprise deals.

CFO Daryl Raiford also added that rising software contributions and stronger customer retention are improving margins.

The company also highlighted major wins, including a U.S. financial services company with ~70M+ active accounts and a major global life insurer migrating to its AI-ready cloud platform.

Moreover, Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts currently maintain around a 4-4.20 buy rating on the stock, while its 3-month momentum grade stands at A+ with 72.98%.

Other communication-service stocks to watch include Lumen Technologies (LUMN), T-Mobile US (TMUS), Cogent Communications (CCOI), Verizon Communications (VZ), Deutsche Telekom (DTEGY), KORE Group Holdings (KORE), Scholastic Corporation (SCHL), and Swisscom AG (SCMWY).
